groups.title: Student Accommodation
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the table below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

table.title: Student Accommodation
content:
Standard: £3276 per year
en suite: £$1$ per year
(meals are not included)

36 weeks
(Rooms are not available during the $2$)

Private renting:
Room in a shared house
or a $5$

From £275 to £490 per month
Additional costs (for this year)
• gas and electricity:
  approximately £$6$
  per month
• $7$: £9
• $8$

questions.title: Room in a shared house or a 【   】

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: College Accommodation Office, Darren speaking, how may I help you?

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Oh, good morning.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: I am starting at the college in September and need to find some accommodation.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Well, there is a list of options.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: The first one is to live on campus.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: And that'd be a single room.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: How much would that cost?

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Well, it depends.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: You'll be expected to cover 3276 pounds for a standard room for the year.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: That's just for a room with a washbasin.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: And it'll be 3834 pounds if you want a regular suite equipped with a small bathroom.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: I see.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: That's a bit over what I expected.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: It does include heating, which is quite a saving because energy costs can be high these days, especially in winter.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Hum, does it include meals as well?

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: No.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: All our rooms are self-catering now.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: But there is a shared kitchen on each floor where you can cook if you want to.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Or there are plenty of places to eat out on campus.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Ok.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: And you mentioned that is the price for the whole year?

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Well, you pay annually.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: But actually, it's just for 36 weeks.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: It doesn't include holidays.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: You'll have to vacate the room then.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Oh, hum, I need somewhere for the holidays.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: It'll be a long way home, and the flights are too expensive.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Well, there is another option.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Several families who work at the college or have children studying here offer overseas students a room in their homes.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Oh, tell me about it.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: We call this arrangement 'Home Welcome'.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: And we've still got a few places left at the moment.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: You pay 150 pounds per week and that includes breakfast, a packed lunch and dinner, as well as heating.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Is there a contract?

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Do you have to stay for the whole year?

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: No, it's flexible.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Hmm, it sounds perfect, but I'm not sure.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: I want to be a little more independent.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Is there somewhere available off campus?

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Yes, you can rent a property in town privately.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: You might want to rent a room in a shared house.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: You'd have a room and a shared kitchen and bathroom with other tenants.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: What about if I don't want to share?

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: You can get what we call a studio.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: They are often relatively small, but they'll have everything you need.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: How much would those two options be?

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Well, prices vary depending on which part of the town the property is in.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Generally speaking, the cheapest is around 275 pounds a month for each student.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Oh, that's not too bad.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Yes, but it can also be up to 490 pounds, and you still have to pay all your other bills.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: What sort of amount would I be looking at for those?

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Last year, students were paying, on average, about 43 pounds per month for gas and electricity.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: This year, it would probably be somewhere in the region of 48 pounds.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: That's a lot.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Yes, they've gone up quite a bit.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: And on top of that, you have to pay for water, which would probably be around 9 pounds.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: That didn't occur to me.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: And I guess I'd have to pay for transportation, too.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: That's right.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Most of these properties are quite a long way from the college.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Oh, it just gets worse and worse.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: What's the minimum contract for this type of accommodation?

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: Six months.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: And you have to pay a deposit upfront.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: That's true.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: And you'd have to provide references.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: They want two from someone in this country.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: I see.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: That's not a problem.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: If you prefer this accommodation, we can't find the property for you.

transcript.speaker: Darren
transcript.en: You'd have to contact the real estate agent who manages the property for the owner.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Thanks.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: I'd like to see what's available privately.

transcript.speaker: Woman
transcript.en: Could you give me the real estate agent details ...

1.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 £3276、en suite；提示信息为 £、per year，此处应填每年的住宿费用，且符合 en suite 条件。
题目解析：定位词 3276 pounds 先出现，且搭配题干 standard 这一要求。接着往下听，听到数字 3834 先记录下来，随后又听到 with a small bathroom，刚好是题干 en suite 的同义替换，由此可确认答案为 3834。注意本题题干 en suite 这个表达较难，但是找到 en suite 并不是做题的必要条件，因为除数字 3276 之外，在本题范围内文中只提供了一个数字，记录下来即为正确答案。

2.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 36 weeks；提示词为 not、during，此处应填负向信息搭配的时间类名词或名词短语。
题目解析：定位词 36 weeks 先出现，随后马上出现否定句，搭配符合预判的时间类信息 holidays，完全符合命题要求，因此可填写答案。

3.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 family；提示词为 room，此处应填住宿类名词或名词短语。
题目解析：定位词 families 出现较早，由 Darren 提及，随后女生提问想进一步了解情况，因此 Darren 继续补充说他们称这种安排为 Home Welcome，这符合预判答案方向。注意，听力答案大小写都可以，不会影响评分。

4.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 contract、arrangements；提示词为 are，此处词性无法准确预判，选取搭配合约、协议类信息填写。
题目解析：定位词 contract 由女生提出，随后 Darren 回答 flexible，意为“灵活的”，与题干 arrangements are 搭配，意为“合约很灵活（不需要住一整年）”，符合命题要求。注意，表格题的表头信息一定不要漏读，有时会提供重要信息。

5.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 private renting、shared house；提示词为 a，此处应填住宿类名词或名词短语。
题目解析：定位词 rent a property in town privately 先出现，随后出现另一个定位词 shared house。此时不要听到一个名词就填写下来，注意等符合预判方向的信息。题干 shared house 被具体展开描述：有独立房间，但需要共享厨房和浴室。耐心听下去，可知 Darren 随后提及的 studio，才是和 shared house 相呼应的住宿类型。注意，做填空题时，不要盯着题干结构做题，做题的本质在于信息而非语法结构或语序。

6.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 gas and electricity；提示词为 £，此处应填每月燃气费和电费的金额总和。
题目解析：定位词 gas and electricity 前出现的第一个数字 43 pounds 为去年的金额，继续往下听，听到强调今年的数据为 48 英镑。注意熟悉数字类考点常见的干扰形式：时间、态度、程度等。

7.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 £9；提示词为 gas and electricity，此处应填与燃气费、电费同类型的其他住宿相关开销项目。
题目解析：上一题通过女生评价 “That's a lot.” 而结束。接下来，可以抓取符合本题预判的信息。先听到 water，符合预判，随后听到定位信息 £9，进一步确认答案。

8.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 gas and electricity，此处应填与燃气费、电费同类型的其他住宿相关开销项目。
题目解析：上一题彻底结束后，开始抓听符合预判的信息。随后听到 transportation，符合预判，可以填写。本题最大的障碍可能是语境认知问题，没有该类生活经验的同学可能会觉得交通费和房租没有关系，故而不填。但房屋位置会决定交通成本，因此这也是一个需要考虑的租房因素。

9.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 6 months；提示词为 need，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：定位词以 six months 的形式出现，接着出现 deposit 一词，该词意为“押金”，符合预判，可以填写。注意积累住宿场景相关词汇，避免听出却写不出的情况。

10.analysis:
听前预测：定位词、提示词为 two，此处应填复数名词或中性名词复数短语。
题目解析：上一题通过女生评价 “That's true.” 而结束。接下来，可以抓听符合预判的信息。先听到 references，符合预判，继而听到定位信息 two，进一步确认答案。本题的做题障碍可能在词汇和语境的认知方面上。其一，reference 是个多义词，结合上下文才能推出其在此意为“推荐信，介绍信”；其二，许多中国学生可能没有碰到过拿着介绍信租房的情况。因此，平时需注意总结听力考试所涉及的语境问题，强化同一类语境的横向梳理。
